metadata.title: Order-Of-Magnitude Invariance Across 47 Orders of Scale
metadata.creator: Daniel Alexander Trawin

master_row.title: Order-Of-Magnitude Invariance Across 47 Orders of Scale
master_row.canonical_statement: The unification theory predicts that properly normalised physical quantities maintain similar magnitudes over an enormous range of scales, highlighting deep structural consistency.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an inequality / bound relating Q.
master_row.dictionary: For quantities (Q) and (Q') related by the scaling functor between laboratory and astrophysical systems, the ratio (QQ') remains within a single order of magnitude across 47 orders of magnitude in size.
master_row.encyclopedia: The scaling relation is valid when corresponding quantities remain order-equivalent despite extreme differences in physical size. Interpretive role: This principle is the registry-level justification for publishing a single comparative canon instead of unrelated scale-specific notebooks. It says that once the correct normalization is chosen, the same structure remains visible from laboratory systems to astrophysical ones.
